evidence_quotes.quote: there is still an extra shop in the game where you can spend more money
it is so designed that you are more tempted to spend money.
extra shop in the game where you can spend more money
there is no guarantee that you can continue to play the game.
Many games are simply destroyed virtually by big companies on the grounds that you never had the game and just buy a licencing for the game.

paragraphs.text: It would be very important to me that the games nowadays remain the right product. The quality of games and customer-friendliness vis-à-vis players appears to be falling sharply over the years. Nowadays you often buy a game for the full price and then find out that there is still an extra shop in the game where you can spend more money and, of course, it is so designed that you are more tempted to spend money. In addition, there is no guarantee that once you have bought the game at the full price and possibly still additional content, there is no guarantee that you can continue to play the game. Many games are simply destroyed virtually by big companies on the grounds that you never had the game and just buy a licencing for the game. We are in an age where physical media have almost been completely replaced. Be it games, music or films. But this makes it increasingly difficult to guarantee that these media are preserved. As a consumer, I simply want to buy a game and then have it without having to make sure that my game can be destroyed at any time.
